title: Wire parsed ESP32 BLE values into dashboard cards and chart

requirements.text: In `frontend/src/utils/bleNus.ts`, ensure the ESP32 pipe-delimited BLE string parser splits the incoming string by `|`, then extracts the numeric value after `BAT:`, `LAT:`, and `VAL:` prefixes respectively, returning them as structured fields (e.g., `{ battery: number, latency: number, val: number }`). The parser must handle the exact format `BAT:85|LAT:2050|VAL:450`.

quotes: Split the incoming BLE string by the | character.
My device sends data as: BAT:85|LAT:2050|VAL:450

acceptanceCriteria: Parser splits on `|` before extracting each field.
Returns numeric types, not strings, for all three fields.

requirements.text: In `frontend/src/contexts/BluetoothContext.tsx`, update the BLE notification handler so that when a notification arrives it invokes the pipe-delimited parser from `bleNus.ts` and stores the resulting `battery`, `latency`, and `val` fields in the context state, replacing any prior ad-hoc parsing of these values.

quotes: Split the incoming BLE string by the | character.

acceptanceCriteria: On receiving a BLE notification with the format `BAT:85|LAT:2050|VAL:450`, the context updates its `battery`, `actuationLatency`, and `lightLevel` (or equivalent `val`) state fields.
No stale or hardcoded fallback values remain in the notification handler path.

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/components/device/BatteryIndicator.tsx` to consume the `battery` value (0–100) from `BluetoothContext` and display it in the Battery card. The card must show the numeric percentage and update live as new BLE notifications arrive.

quotes: Extract the number after BAT: and display it in the Battery card.

acceptanceCriteria: Battery card shows the integer parsed from `BAT:` in the BLE string.
When the BLE value is 85, the card displays `85%`.
The progress bar and color-coded icon reflect the live value.

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/components/device/ActuationLatencyCard.tsx` to consume the `latency` value (milliseconds integer) from `BluetoothContext` and display it in the Actuation Latency card. The card must show the live value as new BLE notifications arrive.

quotes: Extract the number after LAT: and display it in the Actuation Latency card.

acceptanceCriteria: Actuation Latency card shows the integer parsed from `LAT:` in the BLE string.
When the BLE value is 2050, the card displays `2050 ms`.
The displayed value updates live as new notifications arrive.

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/components/charts/BlinkRateChart.tsx` to feed the `val` field (light level) from `BluetoothContext` into the real-time light level chart. Each incoming BLE notification should append a new data point (timestamp + `val` value) to the chart's rolling data series.

quotes: Use the number after VAL: for the real-time light level chart.

acceptanceCriteria: The real-time light level chart plots data points sourced from the `VAL:` field of each BLE notification.
New data points are appended with the current timestamp on each notification.
The chart updates visually as new BLE values arrive.

constraints: Do not modify any file under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All BLE parsing logic must remain in frontend/src/utils/bleNus.ts; do not duplicate it in components.
Do not change the backend schema or add any new backend endpoints.
nonGoals: Adding new BLE characteristics or changing the BLE connection flow.
Persisting parsed BAT/LAT/VAL values to the ICP backend canister.
Changing the visual design or layout of the dashboard beyond populating existing empty cards.
